template_description: Default template for oncological data extraction - 22 clinical entities

entities.name: GRNumdos
entities.definition: Unique patient identification number in the system
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract the patient identification number, usually composed of digits and/or letters

entities.name: Name
entities.definition: Patient's current family name
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract the patient's family name as it appears on the document

entities.name: Birth Name
entities.definition: Patient's family name at birth, if different from current name
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract the patient's birth name if mentioned and different from current name

entities.name: First Name
entities.definition: Patient's first name(s)
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract the patient's first name(s) as they appear on the document

entities.name: Gender
entities.definition: Patient's gender
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract the patient's gender: Female, Male

entities.name: Date of Birth
entities.definition: Patient's complete date of birth
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract the date of birth in Day/Month/Year format (DD/MM/YYYY)

entities.name: Hospital
entities.definition: Reference hospital establishment
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract the hospital name

entities.name: Cancer Locations
entities.definition: Anatomical location of the primary tumor (primary cancer site).
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract the location of the PRIMARY tumor. Look for expressions like 'primary tumor', 'primary cancer', 'primary site', 'cancer origin'. Ignore metastases, secondary locations, or disseminations.
entities.aggregation_instructions: If multiple mentions of the primary location are found, return the most frequently mentioned or most recently confirmed. Map to a single value among: [list of valid_values]. If no clear primary location, return 'Not determined'.

entities.name: Primary Tumor Comment
entities.definition: Clinical synthesis of primary tumor history, evolution and treatments received.
entities.extraction_instructions: Create a chronological synthesis focused on: initial diagnosis, tumor characteristics, oncological treatments, therapeutic responses, disease progressions. EXCLUDE palliative care details, family information, and non-oncological aspects.
entities.aggregation_instructions: Structure chronologically: 1) Initial diagnosis and characteristics, 2) Adjuvant treatments, 3) Relapses and metastatic treatments, 4) Disease evolution. Maintain oncological focus. Synthesize in short and clear paragraphs.

entities.name: Molecular Abnormality
entities.definition: All molecular alterations detected in the tumor, including genetic mutations, losses, amplifications, variants of unknown significance and specific biomarkers.
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract all molecular abnormalities, genetic mutations, gene losses, amplifications, variants of unknown significance (VUS), and biomarker results reported in clinical sources. Include gene name, type of alteration, and any notes on clinical significance if available.
entities.aggregation_instructions: Group and list each unique molecular abnormality in a structured way (for example: gene, type of alteration, functional status). Organize the list by type of alteration (mutations, amplifications, losses, others). Eliminate all redundancy and ensure clarity and readability of results.

entities.name: Metastatic
entities.definition: Indicates if cancer presents metastases based on documented evidence and includes verbatim text used for this conclusion.
entities.extraction_instructions: Look for explicit evidence of metastases in text: mentions of 'metastases', 'metastatic', 'M1', 'dissemination', 'secondary locations', or specific organs with metastases. Extract exact text containing this information.
entities.aggregation_instructions: If evidence of metastases is found, return 'Yes' followed by verbatim text in quotes, for example: 'Yes - "Presence of multiple liver metastases"'. If multiple references exist, include them all separated by semicolons. If no explicit evidence is found, return 'Information not available in reviewed documents'.

entities.name: Metastatic Site
entities.definition: Metastatic sites and their clinical evolution over time.
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract metastatic sites with their clinical evolution (progression, stability, regression) and dates if available. Include new lesions, progression, regression.
entities.aggregation_instructions: Format: [Date] - [Site]: [Evolution/Description]. Classify chronologically. If no date, group under 'Documented sites'. Merge identical mentions of the same site.

entities.name: Histological Types
entities.definition: Precise histological classification of the tumor according to its cellular type
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract the specific histological type of the tumor as mentioned in the pathology report
entities.aggregation_instructions: Keep all mentioned histological types, removing duplicates, organized by reverse chronological order (most recent first), prioritizing the most recent and detailed

entities.name: Location
entities.definition: Precise anatomical location of the tumor
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract the detailed anatomical location of the primary tumor

entities.name: Diagnosis Date
entities.definition: Official date of cancer diagnosis.
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract the date explicitly associated with cancer diagnosis, looking for expressions like 'cancer diagnosed on', 'diagnosed on', or 'diagnosis made on', in day/month/year format. Ignore birth dates and any dates without clear link to diagnosis.
entities.aggregation_instructions: If multiple dates explicitly linked to cancer diagnosis are found, return the earliest one.

entities.name: Chemotherapy Performed
entities.definition: Chemotherapy treatments previously administered to the patient
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract all details of chemotherapies performed, including protocols, dates and results if mentioned
entities.aggregation_instructions: Analyze all mentions of chemotherapies and create a deduplicated chronological list. Eliminate exact duplicates and consolidate similar protocols. Organize by reverse chronological order (most recent first). Format: • Protocol/drug (date) - indication/context if relevant. Include details of cycles, clinical trials and administration modalities.

entities.name: Radiotherapy Performed
entities.definition: Radiotherapy treatments previously administered to the patient
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract all details of radiotherapies performed, including location, doses and dates if mentioned
entities.aggregation_instructions: Analyze all mentions of radiotherapies and create a deduplicated chronological list. Eliminate exact duplicates and consolidate similar treatments. Organize by reverse chronological order (most recent first). Format: • Type of radiotherapy (date) - location/indication if relevant. Include details of doses, durations and specific modalities.

entities.name: Surgery Performed
entities.definition: Surgical interventions previously performed on the patient with clinical descriptions.
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract surgical interventions with understandable clinical descriptions (e.g.: 'mastectomy', 'resection', 'ablation', 'clearance'). IGNORE CCAM codes, technical codes, and alphanumeric references. Look for descriptions in clear medical language. Include date and anatomical location if mentioned.
entities.aggregation_instructions: Extract only surgeries with clear clinical descriptions. Format: [Date] - [Surgical description] ([Location]). Ignore all technical codes (CCAM, alphanumeric). Remove duplicates. Sort by reverse chronological order. If only technical codes are present, return 'Surgeries documented only by technical codes - clinical details not available'.

entities.name: General Status (WHO)
entities.definition: Patient's general status score according to the World Health Organization scale (0-4)
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract the WHO general status score, usually expressed as a number from 0 to 4

entities.name: G8 Score
entities.definition: G8 geriatric assessment score for patients over 70 years old (score out of 17).
entities.extraction_instructions: Look explicitly for 'G8 Score', 'G8', or 'G8 geriatric assessment' followed by a numerical value (e.g.: 'G8: 12/17', 'G8 Score = 15'). Also extract any mention of absence of G8 assessment or reasons why it was not performed.
entities.aggregation_instructions: If a G8 score is found, return 'G8 Score: [value]/17'. If no score is documented but patient is over 70 years, return 'G8 Score not documented (eligible patient)'. If patient is under 70 years, return 'G8 Score not applicable (patient < 70 years)'. If an alternative assessment is mentioned, indicate it.

entities.name: Notable Personal History
entities.definition: Significant personal medical history of the patient (excluding current cancer)
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract ONLY clinically significant personal medical history DISTINCT from the currently treated cancer. INCLUDE: previous other cancers, chronic diseases impacting prognosis/treatment (diabetes, heart/kidney/liver failure, COPD), major surgical interventions, drug allergies. EXCLUDE: isolated benign symptoms, routine consultations, normal examinations, details of current cancer.
entities.aggregation_instructions: Create a list prioritized by clinical importance: 1) Other previous cancers (with dates) 2) Major comorbidities impacting prognosis 3) Drug allergies 4) Major surgical interventions. Eliminate non-essential details. Format: short points, one line per significant history.

entities.name: Family History
entities.definition: Relevant family medical history for the oncological context.
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract ONLY clinically relevant family pathologies: family cancers, hereditary syndromes, hereditary genetic mutations. PRIORITY to cancers of the same type or predisposing syndromes. Look for 'father/mother/brother/sister with', 'family history of cancer', 'family history'. EXCLUDE non-oncological family pathologies unless directly related to cancer risk.
entities.aggregation_instructions: Prioritize by oncological relevance: 1) Cancers of the same type in close relatives 2) Other family cancers 3) Hereditary syndromes/mutations. Format: [Relationship] - [Pathology] (age if mentioned). If no relevant history, return 'No documented oncological family history'.
